steps.title: Spot the complex series
steps.content: The sentence lists three destinations: Wellington, Walgett, and Moree. Each destination includes a 'where' phrase with its own comma, so semicolons should separate the list items.

steps.title: Reject weak separators
steps.content: Choice A lacks the semicolon before the third destination and misplaces one before the final 'where' phrase. Choice C uses only a comma before the last item. Choice D uses a colon inside the series. The remaining choice keeps the complex series consistently separated. The answer is B.
